meanings.usage: 修饰形容词或分词，表示程度
常用于被动语态，表示颜色深浅

meanings.meaning: 观察力敏锐的；善于观察的
meanings.usage: 描述人具有敏锐的观察能力
指有香味的产品或气味
描述非常奇特、不寻常的事物
形容行为或要求过于极端
描述虚构的、不真实的东西
指混合多种东西制成的物品

examples.en: He keeps a large bottle on permanent display in his study.
examples.zh: 他在书房里永久陈列着一个大瓶子。

examples.en: Despite the fact that the bottle is tinted green, an observant visitor would notice it's filled with greyish substance.
examples.zh: 尽管瓶子被染成绿色，敏锐的来访者会注意到里面装的是灰色物质。

examples.en: When Harry put on his act of being mildly annoyed, the assistant promised to order some for him.
examples.zh: 当哈利装出有点烦恼的样子时，店员答应为他订购一些。

examples.en: The woman's eyes immediately lit up and she fetched several bottles.
examples.zh: 那女人的眼睛立刻亮了起来，拿出了好几个瓶子。

examples.en: He beat a hasty retreat, clutching the precious bottle under his arm.
examples.zh: 他匆忙撤退，腋下夹着那个珍贵的瓶子。

examples.en: He was glad to get away with a mere twenty pounds.
examples.zh: 他很高兴只花了二十英镑就买到了。

examples.en: He is fond of relating this interesting story.
examples.zh: 他喜欢讲述这个有趣的故事。

examples.en: He would invent fanciful names on the spot.
examples.zh: 他会当场编造一些稀奇古怪的名字。

examples.en: None of them met with Harry's requirements.
examples.zh: 没有一个能满足哈利的要求。

examples.en: How Harry came into the possession of this outlandish stuff makes an interesting story.
examples.zh: 哈利是如何得到这种稀奇古怪的东西的，这本身就是一个有趣的故事。

grammar.title: 过去完成进行时 (Past Perfect Continuous Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示在过去某一时刻之前一直在进行的动作，强调动作的持续性和未完成性
grammar.usage: 用于描述持续到过去某一点的习惯性动作

examples.en: He had been developing a bad habit for years.
examples.zh: 他多年来一直养成一个坏习惯。

grammar.title: if虚拟条件句（与现在事实相反）
grammar.definition: 表示与现在事实相反或不太可能发生的情况
grammar.structure: If + 主语 + were to + 动词原形...
grammar.usage: 用于假设不太可能发生的事情

examples.en: If you were to ask Harry what was in the bottle, he would tell you that it contained perfumed mud.
examples.zh: 如果你问哈利瓶子里是什么，他会告诉你里面装的是香水泥。

grammar.title: 间接引语（陈述句的转换）
grammar.definition: 将直接引语转换为间接引语时，人称、时态、时间状语等需要相应变化
grammar.usage: 叙述他人说过的话时使用，that可省略

examples.en: He said (that) the bottle contained perfumed mud.
examples.zh: 他说瓶子里装的是香水泥。

sentencePatterns.pattern: If you were to do..., would/could/might + 动词原形...

original.en: If you were to ask Harry what was in the bottle, he would tell you that it contained perfumed mud.
original.zh: 如果你问哈利瓶子里是什么，他会告诉你里面装的是香水泥。

imitations.en: If you were to visit China, you would learn a lot about its culture.
imitations.zh: 如果你要去中国旅游，你会学到很多关于中国文化的知识。

imitations.en: If you were to practice speaking English every day, you could improve quickly.
imitations.zh: 如果你每天练习说英语，你就能快速进步。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Despite the fact that + 从句...

original.en: Despite the fact that the bottle is tinted a delicate shade of green, an observant visitor would soon notice that it is filled with what looks like a thick, greyish substance.
original.zh: 尽管瓶子被染成淡绿色，敏锐的来访者很快就会注意到里面装的是看起来像浓稠的灰色物质。

imitations.en: Despite the fact that he is very young, he speaks English fluently.
imitations.zh: 尽管他很年轻，他的英语说得很流利。

imitations.en: Despite the fact that it was raining heavily, we went hiking.
imitations.zh: 尽管雨下得很大，我们还是去徒步了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + love/like + to be told that...

original.en: He loved to be told that one of his imaginary products was temporarily out of stock.
original.zh: 他喜欢听别人说他想象中的产品暂时缺货。

imitations.en: She loves to be told that her cooking is delicious.
imitations.zh: 她喜欢听别人夸她的厨艺好。

imitations.en: Children love to be told that they are clever.
imitations.zh: 孩子们喜欢听别人夸他们聪明。

original.en: How Harry managed to keep a straight face during these performances is quite beyond me.
original.zh: 哈利在这些表演中是怎样忍住不笑的，我实在想不通。

imitations.en: Why she married him is quite beyond me.
imitations.zh: 她为什么嫁给他，我实在想不通。

imitations.en: How he passed the exam is quite beyond me.
imitations.zh: 他是怎么通过考试的，我实在想不通。
